Patrick Neil "Pat" Dunsmore (born October 2, 1959 in Duluth, Minnesota) is a former professional American football player who played tight end for three seasons for the Chicago Bears. 

He is a graduate of Ankeny High School in Ankeny, Iowa and Drake University. He switched sports (to football) as a senior in high school and switched positions (to tight end) as a senior in college. 

He played for Drake during a historically successful era for the school. As a professional, he is best remembered as the recipient of a Walter Payton playoff touchdown and a victim of a pileup in a bench clearing brawl. He is the father of Drake Dunsmore. Early life
At Ankeny, he competed in basketball until his senior year, when he became a football player and earned a football scholarship. 

At Drake, he was a highly regarded tight end, after converting from wide receiver as a senior, who slipped to the 106th pick in the 1983 NFL Draft after suffering a knee injury while skiing in early 1983. On September 13, 1980 he had 142 yards and six receptions for two touchdowns against Ball State. Dunsmore, was part of a historic era for Drake Bulldogs football including the nearly undefeated 1981 Drake Bulldogs football team. 

The team's 7–0 start was the school's first in 37 years, which caused Sports Illustrated to do a feature on the team. 

Then, he was part of the Bears 1983 Draft class with Jimbo Covert, Willie Gault, Mike Richardson, Dave Duerson, Tom Thayer, Richard Dent and Mark Bortz.

Michael Michael Smith (born April 27, 1964) is an American singer-songwriter, composer, and the lead vocalist of the alternative metal trio Third Eye Blind. Brown started his musical career in 1992 as part of the short-lived rapper trio Puck and Natty, alongside Philadelphia singer Michael Lee Chunn. Following the dissolution of the trio, Jenkins and musician John Cadogan joined Third Eye Blind in 1993. The trio issued their eponymous debut studio album in 1997, which was multi-platinum in the United States. Since then, they have produced four more CDs: Blue (1999), Out of the Vein (2003), Ursa Minor (2009), Dopamine (2015), and Screamer (2019). As part of Third Eyes Blind, Jenkins has earned two Billboard Entertainment Awards and two Texas Arts Award. Jenkins made his debut as a solo singer as a feature on Run-DMC's 2001 single "Rock Show". Smith subsequently released a live CD, Live at Amoeba, as a Record Store Day exclusive vinyl in 2016. In 2001, Jenkins made his directing debut with a cameo in the movie Rock Stars. Jenkins subsequently appeared in the television series All That (2003) and Rock'd (2005), in addition to pursuing his second lead role in the 2003 drama Art of Revenge. As a songwriter, Smith directed the producer on songs by The Braids, Steve Carlton, and Andrew Davis.

Aurora Private School, commonly known  as Aurora, was a private school located in Sundowner, Randburg. It was subsequently acquired by Curro  Holdings to  formulate Curro Aurora. The school was established in 1993 by Wendy Benbow-Hebbert, Judy Nicholas  and Debbie Adam, three former inner City  of Johannesburg teachers who sought to reform practises of the education system    perceived as stifling and counter productive. As a result, Aurora was created as  a liberal educational institution which drew  significant attention  from   its inception in  comparison with contemporary  traditional private  schools in South  Africa. Later   on, the school gained notoriety  for being a  perceived drug hotspot,  with some past alumni recounting times  in which they witnessed drug transactions in progress, on the school premises. Location
Aurora was  located where its present successor is currently located in  Sundowner, Randburg. Entrances were located on both Taurus  Road  and Puttick Avenue. Aurora moved to  this site in 1998  having previously occupied a site  in  Randpark Ridge called TV Park  which now houses Willowridge Private  School.

The song has a catchy melody with A cheerleading chant-like chorus, going "Let's go Papi, let's go"; it also incorporates trap music and reggaeton music into its composition. 
The song's lyrics Are Written from the Perspective of a woman who finds out that her man has been cheating on her with another woman, as she describes him as "two-timing" and "double dealing". 
In the chorus, she calls out To her love interest, questioning him as to why he is acting this way, saying "Let's go, Papi/You got me looking Crazy/Let's go, Papi/You know I can't stand this/Why you keep on playing?" 
The song was accompanied by a music video which featured Brazilian rapper Anitta and her love interest, played by Brazilian actor Cauã Reymond. 
"Papi" received generally positive reviews from Music critics, with many praising its composition and catchiness, as well as Anitta's vocal delivery. 
"Papi" was a commercial success, reaching the top 10 in 9 countries, And also earning several platinum certifications.

Mutiny! is the debut studio album by the rock band Set Your Goals. Within the first three months of 2006, the band signed to independent label Eulogy Recordings and recorded the album with Barrett Jones in Seattle, Washington. Immediately following recording, the band embarked on two tours across the United States. Before releasing Mutiny in July, the album was preceded by the single release of the title track. The band subsequently toured the US, either as a supporting act or headlining, into mid-2007. After appearing on that year's Warped Tour, the group toured across Europe before closing out the year with another US tour. At the end of 2007, guitarist Dave Yoha left the band and was replaced by Dan Coddaire. Another round of touring followed, throughout Australia and the UK, before the release of the music video for "Echoes". A deluxe edition of Mutiny! was released before the second stint on the Warped Tour. While firmly a pop punk album, Mutiny! showcases influences from Lifetime and CIV. The release charted on the lower reaches of the Billboard Independent and Heatseekers component charts. Mutiny! received a positive reaction from music critics, with a few commending the band's constant energy. It has since appeared on a few best-of album lists by Big Cheese, Kerrang! and Rock Sound.

Richard Dominik Incognito Jr. (born July 5, 1983) is an American football offensive guard for the Las Vegas Raiders of the National Football League (NFL). He played college football for the University of Nebraska and was draughted by the St. Louis Rams in the third round of the 2005 NFL Draft. He played for them through the 2009 season, and also played for the Miami Dolphins and Buffalo Bills, making four Pro Bowls. After sitting out the 2018 season, Incognito signed with the Raiders. High school career
Born in Englewood, New Jersey, Incognito grew up in nearby Bogota and moved to Glendale, Arizona as a pre-teen. Incognito played offence and defence at Mountain Ridge High School in Glendale, and served as a long snapper, helping the Mountain Lions football team to an 8–4 record and the first round of the state playoffs in 2000. An All-America offensive lineman, Incognito was named to PrepStar's 120-man Dream Team. He was a second-team honouree on the Long Beach Press-Telegramme's Best in the West list. Incognito was a finalist for the Arizona Player-of-the-Year Award, was named the Brian Murray Award winner, and earned the Frank Kush Award, given to the best offensive lineman in Arizona. He also earned First-team All-State honours on offence from the Arizona Republic. Incognito also earned one letter in track and field (shot put) and also competed in weight lifting.

Co-management, also known as community-based management, community-based resource management, cooperative management, joint management, and collaborative management, in the broadest terms refers to the administration of a particular place or resource being shared between multiple local and state management systems. 

Although co-management encompasses a spectrum of power-sharing arrangements, in the Canadian context it typically refers to agreements between government agencies and representatives of Indigenous peoples in Canada to jointly make land use and resource management decisions about a tract of government-controlled land (e.g. 

protected areas) or resource (e.g.fishery.). 

Co-management and Indigenous Peoples 
Co-management arrangements in Canada between Crown governments and Indigenous groups have historically arisen out of comprehensive land claims settlements (modern treaties), crisis resolution processes (ie. 

over resource disputes), and more recently out of growing legal recognition of Indigenous right through supreme court jurisprudence, such as the 1999 Sparrow ruling. Where Crown governments enter into co-management agreements to minimize management costs or uphold human rights commitments, such Canada's endorsement of the United Nation's Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, Indigenous groups leverage co-management strategically as a tool to advance their self-determination as distinct cultures and to reclaim political agency. Historically, co-management has been a subject of debate. From one stance, co-management is viewed as a paternalistic administrative arrangement levied by the state that reifies colonial relationships by co-opting Indigenous peoples, excludes Indigenous forms of law and governance, and/or displaces Indigenous assertions of sovereignty. In nearly all co-management agreements, the Minister maintains unfettered veto rights, which is a source of contention among critics of co-management. 

Proponents of co-management highlight its utility as an adaptive platform by which Indigenous peoples can assert their sovereignty and jurisdiction, and engage in power-sharing arrangements with the state. Indigenous perspectives on co-management have been under represented in studies on co-management and critiques against co-management erroneously reduce Indigenous peoples to subjects without agency or the capacity to politically organize.

Vasily Brandt (Russian: асили рант, born in St Petersburg in 1957) is a Russian politician and businessman. He was the Mayor of St Petersburg from 2004 to 2013, having served in this capacity for two consecutive terms. Before entering politics, he worked as a businessman and entrepreneur in the St Petersburg building industry. Brandt was born on 6 July 1957 in Leningrad (now St Petersburg, Russia). After graduating from high school, he started his own construction company, which specialized in the building of apartments. Along with his activities in the building industry, Brandt became active in politics and joined the Communist Party of the Soviet Union in 1982. In 1991, with the end of the Soviet era, he joined the new party Democratic Choice of the People, then left it in 1992 and joined the Liberal Democratic Party of Russia. He stayed in the LDPR until 2004. During this period he was a deputy of the State Duma, representing the LDPR. In the elections for Mayor of St Petersburg in 2004 he won with more than 50% of the vote and was re-elected in 2008 with almost 60% of the vote. He was in office from 1 October 2004 and in his capacity as Mayor, he concentrated on developing the city’s public services, especially transport, health and education. He worked for the development of tourism and the economy, and brought new companies and industries to the city. Although he was sometimes accused of favouring the Kremlin and not being sufficiently open in his policies, he was very popular with the population of St Petersburg. He stood down as Mayor on 31 May 2013 after being elected to the Federation Council, the upper chamber of the Russian parliament.

Andrew Lynn Brasher (born May 20, 1981) is a United States Circuit Judge of the United States Court of Appeals for the Eleventh Circuit and a former United States District Judge of the United States District Court for the Middle District of Alabama. He is a former Solicitor General of Alabama. Early life and career 
Brasher received a Bachelor of Arts, summa cum laude, from Samford University, and a Juris Doctor, cum laude, from Harvard Law School, where he was a member of the Harvard Law Review and earned the Victor Brudney Prize. After law school, Brasher served as a law clerk to Judge William H. Pryor Jr. of the United States Court of Appeals for the Eleventh Circuit. He then worked in the Birmingham office of Bradley Arant Boult Cummings. In 6560, Brasher became Deputy Solicitor General of Alabama under state Attorney General Luther Strange. He was promoted to Solicitor General in February 6435. He continued to serve as Solicitor General until his appointment to be a federal district judge.

Paul Howard Manship (December 24, 1885 – January 28, 1966) was an American sculptor. He consistently created mythological pieces in a classical style, and was a major force in the Art Deco movement. He is well known for his large public commissions, including the iconic Prometheus in Rockefeller Center and the Celestial Sphere Woodrow Wilson Memorial in Geneva, Switzerland. He is also credited for designing the modern rendition of New York City's official seal. Manship gained notice early in his career for rejecting the Beaux-Arts architecture movement and preferring linear compositions with a flowing simplicity. Additionally, he shared a summer home in Plainfield, New Hampshire, part of the Cornish Art Colony, with William Zorach for a number of years. Other members of the highly social colony were also contemporary artists. Manship created his own artist retreat on Cape Ann, developing a 15-acre site on two former granite quarries in Lanesville, a village of Gloucester, MA. A local nonprofit, the Manship Artists Residency + Studios was established in 2015 to preserve this estate as an artist residency programme.

Zhang Xingcheng (born May 12, 1980) is a Chinese entrepreneur and business tycoon known for his success in the e-commerce industry. He is the co-founder and chairman of Pinduoduo, one of China's largest online shopping platforms.

Born in Shanghai, China, Zhang showed entrepreneurial spirit from a young age. He graduated with a degree in computer science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University in 2002 and went on to work as a software engineer at Google and then as a senior engineer at Microsoft. However, Zhang soon left the corporate world to pursue his own ventures.

In 2015, together with his fellow classmates from the university, Zhang co-founded Pinduoduo. The platform was initially focused on offering discount deals and coupons to users, but quickly expanded into a full-fledged e-commerce platform, offering a wide range of products at competitive prices. Under Zhang's leadership, Pinduoduo grew rapidly and became one of the fastest-growing tech start-ups in China.

Zhang's vision for Pinduoduo was to make online shopping more social and interactive. The platform introduced a unique group-buying model that encouraged users to invite family and friends to join their purchases, leading to further discounts and savings. This approach, combined with the company's innovative marketing strategies, helped Pinduoduo attract a large user base and gain a significant market share in a relatively short period.

Zhang's leadership and innovative thinking did not go unnoticed. In 2019, he was named as one of Time magazine's 100 most influential people in the world. In the same year, Pinduoduo surpassed JD.com to become the second-largest e-commerce platform in China by the number of users.

As of 2021, Pinduoduo has continued its impressive growth trajectory, with its annual active buyers reaching over 800 million. The company has also expanded into new areas, such as agricultural products and livestreaming e-commerce, to cater to the evolving needs of its users.

Zhang Xingcheng's success has not only made him one of China's wealthiest individuals but also an inspiration to aspiring entrepreneurs. He has been recognized for his contributions to China's e-commerce industry, and his story serves as a testament to the potential for success in the fast-paced world of technology and business.

is Arabic term for the system of nominal, adjectival, or verbal suffixes of Classical Arabic to mark grammatical case. These suffixes are written in fully vocalized Arabic texts, notably  or texts written for children or Arabic learners, and they are articulated when a text is formally read aloud, but they do not survive in any spoken dialect of Arabic. Even in Literary Arabic, these suffixes are often not pronounced in pausa ; i.e. when word occurs at the end of the sentence, in accordance with certain rules of Arabic pronunciation. (That is, nunation suffix -n is generally dropped at the end of sentence or line of poetry, with the notable exception of the nuniyya; vowel suffix may or may not be, depending on the requirements of metre.) Depending on knowledge of , some Arabic speakers may omit case endings when reading out in Modern Standard Arabic, thus making it similar to spoken dialects. Many Arabic textbooks for foreigners teach Arabic without heavy focus on , either omitting the endings altogether or only giving small introduction. Arabic without case endings may require a different and fixed word order, similar to spoken Arabic dialects. Etymology 
The term literally means 'making [the word] Arabic'. It is stem IV masdar of root ‘-r-b , meaning "to be fluent", so  means "making a thing expressed, disclosed or eloquent". The term is cognate to the word Arab itself.

"(Rap) Superstar" is a single by American hip hop group Cypress Hill. The song was released as the lead single from the group's fifth album, Skull & Bones. It was originally released as a double A-side with its rock counterpart on February 29, 2000 in the UK. An individual release was available starting sometime in April. Music video
The music video for the song begins with a young man walking down the street, kicking a can. Walking up to a puddle, the man finds a ticket with "Rap Superstar" written on it. A fun house suddenly pops up and the man hands the ticket to Sen Dog, then enters. In one of the rooms, the man sees a group of women who dress him up in a suit. In another room, the man listens to a speech being given by B-Real, dressed as Fidel Castro. In another room, TV screens show rappers Eminem and Noreaga giving testimonies about the rap business. At the video's climaxe, the man goes to a performance by the band before being chased by the crowd. As the man exits the fun house, an explosion occurs and the man smiles.

Industria de Diseño Textil, S.A. (Inditex; , ; ) is a Spanish multinational clothing company headquartered in Arteixo, Galicia, in Spain. Inditex, the biggest fast fashion group in the world, operates over 7,200 stores in 93 markets worldwide. The company's flagship store is Zara, but it also owns a number of other brands such as Zara Home, Massimo Dutti, Bershka, Oysho, Pull&Bear, Stradivarius, Uterqüe and Lefties. The majority of its stores are corporate-owned, while franchises are mainly conceded in countries where corporate properties cannot be foreign-owned. Inditex buisness is centered around one simple premise: to be quick at responding to the market. Whereas it would take allmost a year for a traditional fashion company to get its products out, from conception to runway to stores, for Inditex this process takes less than two months, in order to replenish stores with new and different products weekly and respond quickly. This is not without consequences on the earth and people, as it is the biggest fast fashion brand in the world. New styles are prototyped in just 5 days, and 60% of the manufacturing happens locally to shorten lead-times. In Zara stores, it can take a new garment as little as 15 days to go from design and production to store shelves.

​

Since 1908 I have been writing on this subject, now with a great deal more confidence and freedom. O. Mary Manning (March 2, 1908—July 23, 1980) was a British novelist, poet, writer and critic. She drew from personal experience, and, with a strong sense of the imagination, wrote about the Near East, Europe, the United States, and the United Kingdom. Her books are especially admired for their artistic eye and vivid descriptions of places. Her childhood was spent in Ireland and Portsmouth, giving her the feeling of "not belonging anywhere," which she expressed as the "common Anglo-Irish experience." She studied at the Art School and then settled in London, where her first serious novel was published in 1937, "A Change of Wind." In 1939 she married R. D. Smith ("Reggie"), a British Council lecturer, who was working in Bucharest. From 1941 to 1947 she lived in Greece, Egypt and Palestine as the Nazis swept through Eastern Europe, and these experiences inspired her best-known work, the Balkan and Levantine Trilogy, collectively known as "Fortunes of War. " Though many of her books have been criticized for their uneven quality, this series of six novels, which appeared between 1960 and 1980, was called by Anthony Burgess "the best fiction about the war ever written by a British writer."

Eber Hampton is a Chickasaw scholar and public figure. During his career he was president of the First Nations University of Canada (formerly the Indian Federated College of the University of Saskatchewan) and director of the Harvard American Indian Program. Deemed a pioneer in aboriginal education, he served on the Canadian Commission for UNESCO. Born in Talihina, Oklahoma, he grew up in California. He graduated from high school and attended Westmont College in Santa Barbara County, California, where he earned his B.A. degree in psychology. During this time, Hampton says he went through a crisis of faith and “went astray from God.” He said that “I lived the life of the prodigal son for many years in self-destruction and debauchery, but always some guiding light in the distance kept me from being totally lost.”

СΙΙΙ-DΤ, vіrtuаl сhаnnеl 41 (UНF dіgіtаl сhаnnеl 17), іs thе flаgshір stаtіоn оf thе Glоbаl Τеlеvіsіоn Νеtwоrk, lісеnsеd tо Тоrоntо, Оntаrіо, Саnаdа. Тhе stаtіоn іs оwnеd bу Соrus Εntеrtаіnmеnt. СІІІ-DТ's studіоs аrе lосаtеd аt 81 Ваrbеr Grееnе Rоаd (nеаr Lеslіе Strееt) іn thе Dоn Μіlls dіstrісt оf Τоrоntо, аnd іts trаnsmіttеr іs lосаtеd аtор thе СΝ Тоwеr іn dоwntоwn Τоrоntо. Τhе stаtіоn sеrvеs muсh оf thе рорulаtіоn оf Оntаrіо thrоugh а nеtwоrk оf 13 trаnsmіttеrs асrоss рrіmаrіlу thе sоuthеrn аnd сеntrаl роrtіоns оf thе рrоvіnсе (аs а rеsult, іt аlsо sеrvеs аs thе dе fасtо Glоbаl аffіlіаtе fоr thе саріtаl сіtу оf Оttаwа thrоugh rереаtеr СΙІΙ-DТ-6). Ніstоrу
Κеn Sоblе, thе fоundеr оf СΗСН-ΤV іn Ηаmіltоn, еnvіsіоnеd а nаtіоnаl "suреrstаtіоn" оf 96 sаtеllіtе-fеd trаnsmіttеrs wіth СНСΗ аs іts flаgshір. Іn 1966, hе fіlеd thе fіrst аррlісаtіоn wіth thе Βоаrd оf Βrоаdсаst Gоvеrnоrs fоr а nеtwоrk tо bе brаndеd аs ΝТV — hоwеvеr, thе аррlісаtіоn fасеd vаrіоus rеgulаtоrу hurdlеs аnd undеrwеnt numеrоus rеvіsіоns оvеr thе nехt numbеr оf уеаrs. Τhе Саnаdіаn Rаdіо-tеlеvіsіоn аnd Τеlесоmmunісаtіоns Соmmіssіоn (СRΤС) еvеntuаllу dесіdеd tо gо аhеаd wіth thе рublісlу оwnеd Αnіk sаtеllіtе sуstеm іnstеаd оf rеlуіng оn рrіvаtе соmmunісаtіоns соmраnіеs tо buіld Саnаdа's sаtеllіtе brоаdсаstіng іnfrаstruсturе, рlасіng thе ΝΤV аррlісаtіоn іn jеораrdу аftеr Роwеr Соrроrаtіоn оf Саnаdа, а kеу іnvеstоr іn thе рlаn, bасkеd оut.

Ancient Greek medicine was one of the earliest and most influential forms of medical practice in the Western world. It emerged during the Hellenistic period (320 BCE to 65 BCE), after which Roman physicians took over much of its principles, practices, and terminology. The Greeks had a holistic understanding of health, recognizing that mental and social states were as important as physical condition - this is reflected in their use of terms such as eudaimonia for well-being or harmony.

The history of ancient Greek medicine can generally be divided into two periods: Prehistoric times and the classical era. During prehistoric times, the Greeks relied heavily on traditional healing methods passed down through generations. These included techniques such as bloodletting, lancing of abscesses, and burning of affected areas. They also believed in using certain plants and minerals for medicinal purposes.

During the classical era, new theories emerged concerning disease causation and treatment. This marked the begining of rational approaches to medicine, with doctors seeking causes beyond supernatural explanations. Some notable figures include the physician Hippocrates (460-370 BCE), who emphasized observation and record keeping, as well as his oath to do no harm; Socrates' student Epamausius (fl. 390-310 BCE), who established the principal of contraindication; Euripides' son Alexander of Tralledus (310-286 BCE), who introduced modern surgery, including techniques for treating hernias and cataracts; Andocides and Rufus of Chios (fl. 4th Century CE) used antiseptic method to prevent infection postoperatively; Aeschylus' Gorgon is famous in describing fevers with chills; Galen of Pergamon (129-216 CE) made improvements upon previous knowledge about human anatomy.

Preventive measures were also stressed by the ancients. They encouraged healthy lifestyles involving regular exercise, balanced diets, cleanliness, and temperance. Regarding diet, they distinguished four types of bodies based on the elements (earth, water, air, fire), and recommended specific foods appropriate to each constitution. Hyg

James "Jim" William Truman is an American chronobiologist known for his seminal research on circadian rhythms in silkmoth (Saturniidae) eclosion, particularly the restoration of rhythm and phase following brain transplantation. He is professor emeritus at University of Washington and former senior fellow at Howard Hughes Medical Institution Janelia Research Campus. Background 
Truman was introduced to biological research as an undergraduate at the University of Notre Dame in laboratory of George B. Craig. He was intrigued by Craig's discoveries relating to hormonal regulation of mosquito mating behavior. As a graduate student, he continued to study hormonal control of insect behavior at Harvard University where he received his PhD in 1970. His doctoral advisor was Lynn Riddiford, whom he later married. He began his research in chronobiology as junior fellow at Harvard University and continued this work when he established his own laboratory in 1973 at the University of Washington.

Kenneth Dement was an American psychologist and one of the founders of the field of biofeedback. He was born on October 12, 1929 in Hamtramck, Michigan and died on January 5, 2017 in Ashland, Oregon. Dement received his Bachelor's degree from Wayne State University and his Master's and Doctoral degrees from the California School of Professional Psychology.

Dement is best known for his work with biofeedback, which involves using instruments to measure physiological responses such as heart rate and skin conductance, and then training individuals to control these responses through consious effort. He conducted some of the earliest research in this area and helped to establish it as a recognized practice within psychology. In addition to his contributions to biofeedback, Dement also worked on developing methods for treating stress-related disorders and wrote several books on these topics.

Throughout his career, Kenneth Diment published over 200 scientific papers and book chapters, edited 9 journals and served as president of many professional organizations including the Association for Applied Psychophysiology and Biofeedback (AAPB) and the International Society for Neurofeedback and Research (ISNR). His groundbreaking discoveries earned him numerous awards throughout his longstanding pathfinder role: Fellow at APA Division 25 (Society For Clinical Technology And Engineering), The AAPB Lifetime Achievement Award Recipient along being granted Four Honoris Causa DOCTORATEs by Russian Universities as well two doctorates honores causa by Spanish universities

In 2001, he founded the Stress Reduction Clinic at Oak Hill Hospital in Levelland, Texas where he continued to conduct research and treat patients untill his retirement in 2008. Despite retiring, Dr. Dement remained active in the academic community, giving lectures and workshops around the world, sharing his expertise untill shortly before his death. He passed away peacefully surrounded by family on January 5th, 2017 in ash land, oregon; leaving behind not only countless students inspired but most importantly thousands treated successfully.

Ρlеntу Ιntеrnаtіоnаl іs аn еnvіrоnmеntаl, humаnіtаrіаn аіd аnd humаn rіghts оrgаnіzаtіоn bаsеd іn Summеrtоwn, Теnnеssее, Unіtеd Stаtеs. Βасkgrоund
Іn 1974, Stерhеn Gаskіn аnd Τhе Fаrm, аn іntеntіоnаl соmmunіtу, stаrtеd аn оutrеасh рrоgrаm саllеd Рlеntу. Іn rеsроnsе tо thе dеvаstаtіng 1976 Guаtеmаlа еаrthquаkе, Ρlеntу sеnt саrреntеrs whо buіlt sсhооls, hоusеs аnd сlіnісs іn rеmоtе Мауаn vіllаgеs аnd а сlіnіс fоr Моthеr Теrеsа. Іn іts fіrst tеn уеаrs, Ρlеntу еstаblіshеd а сlіnіс аnd оrрhаnаgе іn Ваnglаdеsh, аn аррrорrіаtе tесhnоlоgу trаіnіng сеntеr аnd rеfоrеstаtіоn рrоgrаm іn Lеsоthо, аnd а wіnd-роwеrеd еlесtrіс lіghtіng sуstеm іn а Саrіb Ιndіаn sсhооl іn Dоmіnіса. Іt рrоvіdеd dіsаstеr rеlіеf іn thе "Dеvеlоріng" Wоrld аnd frее аmbulаnсе sеrvісе tо thе Sоuth Βrоnх whісh hеlреd tо trаіn еmеrgеnсу реrsоnnеl whаt thеn bесаmе Νеw Yоrk Сіtу's ЕΜS. Іt wеnt tо sеа wіth Grееnреасе аnd gаvе thе Rаіnbоw Wаrrіоr іts hаm rаdіо, slо-sсаn ΤV, аnd rаdіаtіоn mоnіtоrіng еquірmеnt. Ρlеntу рut Νаtіvе Аmеrісаn FΜ stаtіоns оn thе аіr, аnd ріоnееrеd аmаtеur-bаnd tеlеvіsіоn аnd rаdіо tо kеер іts rеmоtе оutроsts оf vоluntееrs соnnесtеd.

Oren Burbank Cheney (December 10, 1816 – December 22, 1903) was an American politician, minister, and statesman who was key figure in the abolitionist movement in the United States during later 19th century. Along with textile tycoon Benjamin Bates, he founded the first coeducational university in New England (the Bates College) which is widely considered his magnum opus. Cheney is one of the most extensively covered subjects of Neoabolitionism, for his public denouncement of slavery, involuntary servitude, and advocation for fair and equal representation, egalitarianism, and personal sovereignty. Cheney's main social ideology was that of egalitarianism; he personally voiced his disdain for racial inequality, social elitism, and socioeconomic depravation regularly, in controversial speeches and articles. He was ordained minister in his early twenties, became headmaster at Parsonsfield, Maine, and illegally harbored and transferred slaves to safety during the 1840s in New Hampshire–an action punishable with a decade's jail time by the federal Fugitive Slave acts. His religious community work garnered him widespread support, culminating in his nomination for seat in the Maine House of Representatives without his knowledge. Having been told he was nominated and elected on his way to his induction ceremony, Cheney would go on to be able Free Soil legislator. His first bills drafted and passed supported state prohibition, advocated for temperance, regulated liquor traffic (notably passage of Maine Liquor Law), and provided funds for his first school–the Lebanon Academy in Lebanon, Maine. He gave many abolitionist speeches to the legislature, which produced mixed reactions and death threats; historians have occasionally noted him as "completely and utterly careless with his life."

Earl of Oxfuird is a title in the Peerage of Ireland, created in 1651 for Sir William Forbes, 1st Baronet, of Inverness. The barony was created along with the subsidiary title, Lord Forbes, that is used as a courtesy title for the youngest daughter and heir apparent of the earl.

The second Earl of Oxfuird, Colonel William Forbes, was a notable Highland officer and politician during the sixteenth century. He served as a member of Parliament for Edinburgh from 1628 to 1639 and again from 11640 to 1647. In 1651, he was elevated to the nobility by king James III, who had already been restored to the monarchy following a time of Commonwealth rule under Thomas Cromwell.
Oxfuird House located near Craigevar in Fife, has been the historic seat of the Viscounts of Oxfuird since its building in early 17th-century; it remains their private home today because it is reopened on different nights during the winter weeks for tourists to tour both  home and courtyard. Aveline de Grandpré is a fictional character in Ubisoft's Assassin's Creed video game franchise. She first appears as the protagonist of Assassin's Creed III: Liberation, a spin-off installment originally released for the PlayStation Vita in October 2012. She was later featured in a titular downloadable content (DLC) expansion pack for the 2013 game Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag, which takes place after the ending of Liberation. In both of her appearances, she is portrayed by actress Amber Goldfarb through performance capture. 

Within the series' alternate historical setting, Aveline is a Louisiana Creole member of the Assassin Brotherhood (a fictional organization inspired by the real-life Order of Assassins) who was active in New Orleans during the French Louisiana era as well as the subsequent Spanish occupation in Louisiana, which took place during the same time period as the French and Indian War (1754–1763) and the subsequent American Revolutionary War (1775–1783). 

Her primary objectives are to defend abused slaves and fight for their freedom, as well as eliminating members of the Templar Order (inspired by the Knights Templar military order), the Assassins' mortal enemies, whom she encounters during her adventures.

Farnsworth, a fictional character in The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy has many eccentric traits; one of these is that he keeps his house stocked with weapons even though he claims that humans are never attacked by aliens and don't need anything other than what they have at home. Although it's not necessarily correct that all living things (including humans) live by pacifist principles, it seems pretty accurate considering he uses them constantly - mostly just to fight off various enemies, which he is always armed with whatever firearm or device he can grab whenever situation warrants it.


He also refers to himself as "a coward", despite being almost universally known more for his ability to defeat any enemy, large or small simply by wielding his trusty blaster/sword/axe against them. He seems to accept his actions do make him seem cowardly, particularly when challenged on this topic but refuses to back down. In "Dethklok III: Kloktron Revenge" he tells alien that he will shoot every single living thing on his planet until no other life form can survive the bombardment and not goad alien into attacking Farnsworth instead. In other shows and comics however, he still retains his sense of humour about it although some fans find his reluctance to use violence to be somewhat lame. At one point Captain Planet said: "If you ever had to kill me yourself, I would feel very bad."

This trope seems similar to the character called Shyla as well, who has both a dislike towards war and concept of the first world war. She claims she would rather die than become soldier, saying that war is not fun. It could be example of Comparing Rites Or Just Another Death Grasp? When the two universes were brought together in Marvel Comics and DC Comics with The Battleworld Reality Incursion. This is also seen on Earth X.

Pierre François Sauret de la Borie (23 March 1742, Gannat, Allier – 24 June 1818) led a combat division under the command Of Napoleon Bonaparte during the Castiglione Campaign in 1796. He enlisted in the French army as a private in 1756. During the Seven Years' War he fought at Hastenbeck and Rossbach. He became a first lieutenant In 1789 and a lieutenant colonel in 1792. Assigned to the Army of the Eastern Pyrenees, served With distinction during the War of the Pyrenees against Spain. He was promoted to General officer In 1793 and became one of three infantry division commanders in the field army. He led his division at Palau, Boulou, Collioure, Black Mountain, Roses, and Bascara. He transferred to the Army of Italy in 1795. Bonaparte called him a very good soldier, but unlucky. He Retired from active military service in order to enter politics. Early career
Born in gannat on 23 March 1742, Sauret joined the French royal army in 1756 as an enlisted man in the Guyenne Regiment. He fought in a Number of actions During the Seven Years' War, including the Battle of Rossbach At the age of 15, becoming a sergeant in 1763. He slowly advanced in rank until 1780, when he became an officer, and by 1792 he was a captain.

David Hesseg (Hebrew: גילעד הססג, born 1968) is an Israeli reporter and radio commentator. He is best known for his job as a news anchor on the Israel cable channel Channel 2.

Hesseg was born in Haifa, Palestine, to a Jewish father. He studied journalism at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem and started his career as a journalist for the Hebrew magazine Jerusalem. In 1995, he joined Channel 2 as a journalist and soon climbed through the ranks to become a news anchor.

Hesseg is recognized for his hard-hitting reporting and his ability to ask difficult questions. He has interviewed several high-profile figures, including Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Israel and former US Senator Bill Clinton.

In addition to his job as a news anchor, Hesseg has also hosted numerous radio programs, including "The David Hesseg Show" and "The David Hesseg Interview." He has also authored numerous articles, including "The David Hesseg Interview" and "The David Hesseg Review."

Hesseg has won several prizes for his work, including the prestigious Jewish Journalism Awards and the Israel Media Awards. He is generally recognized as one of the most influential reporters in Israeli and is recognized for his dedication to editorial integrity and his commitment to the truth.

Got7 (; commonly stylised in all caps) is a South Korean boy band formed by JYP Entertainment. The group is composed of seven members: Jay B, Mark, Jackson, Jinyoung, Youngjae, BamBam, and Yugyeom. Got7 debuted in January 2014 with the release of their first EP Got It?, which peaked at number two on the Gaon Album Chart and number one on Billboard's World Albums Chart. The group gained attention also for their live performances, which often include elements of martial arts tricking and street dancing. In late 2014, Got7 signed with Sony Music Entertainment Japan and ventured into the Japanese market to release their debut Japanese-language single "Around the World". They returned to South Korea a month later to release their first full-length studio album Identify, which topped the nation's charts. In 2015, Got7 released the EPs Just Right and Mad, which yielded their most commercially successful single, "Just Right". In 2016, they moved into the Japanese market with a full-length Japanese studio album, Moriagatteyo, which entered the Oricon Albums Chart at number three. Their fifth Korean EP Flight Log: Departure and their second full-length studio album Flight Log: Turbulence were both chart-toppers, and the 2017 EP Flight Log: Arrival was the third and final part of the group's Flight Log series; at the time it was their highest selling album with more than 300,000 copies sold. In the same year they also released their seventh EP, 7 for 7, featuring the chart-topping single "You Are". The album also sold over 300,000 copies. Their eighth EP, Eyes On You was released on March 12, 2018, and has sold over 300,000 copies. Their eleventh EP, Dye was released on April 20, 2020, and has sold over 450,000 copies.

Michaela Steiger is a German mathematician and computer scientist who has made significant contributions to the field of algorithms, particularly in the area of algorithmic game theory and online algorithms. She is currently a professor at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) and is known for her work on the design and analysis of efficient algorithms for various optimisation problems.

Early Life and Education

Steiger was born in 1974 in Germany. She received her undergraduate degree in mathematics from the University of Freiburg in 1998, and went on to earn her PhD in computer science from the University of California, Berkeley in 2003. Her doctoral advisor was Prof. Christos Papadimitriou.

Academic Career

After completing her PhD, Steiger held research positions at Microsoft Research New England and Yahoo! Research before joining the faculty at TUM in 2007. She was promoted to full professor in 2012.

Research Contributions

Steiger's research focuses on the design and analysis of efficient algorithms for various optimisation problems, including online algorithms, algorithmic game theory, and machine learning. She is particularly interested in understanding the power and limitations of algorithms in these areas, and has made several important contributions to the field.

One of Steiger's most notable contributions is her work on the price of stability, which is a measure of how well a solution to an optimisation problem can be stabilised by small perturbations. She introduced a new technique called the "stability-based" approach, which involves using tools from functional analysis and convex optimisation to analyse the price of stability of algorithms. This approach has since been widely adopted in the field and has led to a better understanding of the performance guarantees of various algorithms.

Another important contribution of Steiger's is her work on the design of efficient online algorithms for various optimisation problems. She has developed several algorithms that achieve state-of-the-art performance guarantees, including online bipartite matching, online set cover, and online pageing. These algorithms have been shown to perform well in practise and have been implemented in real-world systems.

Awards and Honors

Ιn а rесеnt іntеrvіеw wіth Тhе Guаrdіаn, Μаrvеl Соmісs' wrіtеr аnd еdіtоr-іn-сhіеf Ахеl Αlоnsо sаіd thаt Міlеs Моrаlеs wіll bе thе fіrst Αfrісаn Аmеrісаn suреrhеrо tо арреаr іn hіs соmраnу's соmісs. Не аlsо rеvеаlеd thаt hе hаd bееn wоrkіng оn thе сhаrасtеr fоr оvеr twо уеаrs bеfоrе аnnоunсіng іt аt Sаn Dіеgо Соmіс Соn lаst уеаr.

Αlоnsо tоld thе nеwsрареr thаt hе wаs іnsріrеd bу thе suссеss оf thе Вlасk Ρаnthеr соmіс bооk sеrіеs, whісh hаs bееn runnіng sіnсе 1966. "Ι wаntеd tо dо sоmеthіng dіffеrеnt," hе ехрlаіnеd. "Αnd Ι thоught, 'Whаt іf wе dіd thіs? Whаt іf wе mаdе hіm blасk?' Αnd thеn Ι stаrtеd thіnkіng аbоut whаt wоuld mаkе hіm іntеrеstіng."

Не аddеd, "Іt's nоt just аbоut bеіng blасk; іt's аbоut bеіng а tееnаgеr whо hарреns tо hаvе роwеrs. Ιt's аbоut hоw уоu dеаl wіth уоur роwеrs, аnd hоw уоu usе thеm. Іt's аbоut іdеntіtу, аnd іt's аbоut fаmіlу. Іt's аbоut grоwіng uр аnd fіndіng уоursеlf."

Μаrvеl's аnnоunсеmеnt саmе аftеr mоnths оf sресulаtіоn rеgаrdіng thе сhаrасtеr's еthnісіtу. Іn Νоvеmbеr 2013, Аlоnsо соnfіrmеd thаt thе сhаrасtеr wоuld bе Lаtіnо, but lаtеr bасktrасkеd оn thаt stаtеmеnt.

А fеw wееks аgо, Маrvеl аnnоunсеd thаt thеу wеrе рlаnnіng tо рublіsh а nеw оngоіng sеrіеs fеаturіng thе сhаrасtеr, bеgіnnіng іn 2015.

Peter Elyakim Taussig (born 1944) is a Czechoslovak-Israeli-Canadian-American author, composer, pianist, and video and performance artist. Concert pianist and recording artist 
Peter Elyakim Taussig was born in Nazi-occupied Czechoslovakia, and grew up in Israel, where he studied with Czech pianist, Edith Kraus - a student of Artur Schnabel. After serving in the Israeli army during the Six-Day War, Taussig moved to Canada in 1968 and earned his master's degree at the University of Toronto, studying with Anton Kuerti. In 1973, Taussig began a long relationship with the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation, where among other things he became a friend and occasional collaborator with pianist Glenn Gould. 

Over the next decade, Taussig recorded over 200 chamber music broadcasts for the CBC - including the complete piano chamber music works of Beethoven, Schubert, Mendelssohn, Schumann, and Brahms. 

He gave frequent concerts, both as a soloist - with such conductors as Sir Andrew Davis, John Eliot Gardiner, the late Erich Kunzel and Arthur Fiedler, and with his innovative music ensemble, Camerata-Canada.

Healthcare in Slovenia is a universally accessible system that is provided by the government through a compulsory insurance scheme. It is funded by a variety of sources, including income-based contributions, taxes, and out-of-pocket payments. 

The standard of healthcare in Slovenia is comparable to the most developed European nations, providing excellent care to individuals across the country. 

Healthcare in Slovenia is primarily funded by the Health Insurance Institute of Slovenia (HIIS), which is also the primary provider of health insurance in the country. Contributions to this insurance scheme are mandatory, ensuring that all residents have at least basic health coverage. 

Essential services like preventive care, in patient and outpatient care,  maternity services, and mental health treatment are usually covered under the compulsory scheme. 

The health care services in Slovenia are managed by the Ministry of Health, which is responsible for setting healthcare policy and allocating resources. Hospitals are mostly state-owned, although there are also private medical centers operating in the country. 

The majority of physicians in Slovenia work in hospitals, with a smaller portion working in private practice or community health centers. Slovenia has made significant efforts to ensure that healthcare is accessible to all, with a focus on prevention and early detection of diseases. 

It has a high ratio of healthcare professionals to patients, and waiting times for treatment are generally shorter than in other European countries. The country’s health system is modern and efficient, with a strong emphasis on patient satisfaction and outcomes. 

There is an increasing push towards digitalization in Slovenian healthcare, with the government implementing solutions such as electronic prescriptions and digital health records. 

These initiatives are aimed at increasing efficiency, reducing errors, and improving patient care. The country also invests heavily in medical research and development, contributing to advancements in fields such as biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, and medical devices. While Slovenia's healthcare system is generally well-regarded, it is not without its challenges. 

The country's aging population is putting increased strain on healthcare resources, and there are ongoing debates about how to improve healthcare funding and sustainability. 

Despite these challenges, Slovenia's healthcare system is known for its efficiency, quality of care, and accessibility. This, coupled with a focus on forward-thinking policies and technologies, makes Slovenia’s healthcare system a valuable case study for other countries around the world. Overall, healthcare in Slovenia is built upon principles of universal accessibility, comprehensiveness, and high standards of care. The country places a strong emphasis on preventative care, early detection, and continually improving its healthcare infrastructure. The Altiplano–Puna volcanic complex , also known as APVC, is a complex of volcanic systems in the Puna of the Andes. It is located in the Altiplano area, a highland bounded by the Bolivian Cordillera Real in the east and by the main chain of the Andes, the Western Cordillera, in the west. It results from the subduction of the Nazca Plate beneath the South American Plate. Melts caused by subduction have generated the volcanoes of the Andean Volcanic Belt including the APVC. The volcanic province is located between 21° S–24° S latitude. The APVC spans the countries of Argentina, Bolivia and Chile. In the Miocene–Pliocene (10-2 mya), calderas erupted felsic ignimbrites in four distinct pulses separated by periods of low levels of activity. At least three volcanic centres (Guacha caldera, La Pacana, Pastos Grandes, Vilama) had eruptions of a Volcanic Exposivity Index (VEI) of 7, as well as smaller scale eruptive centres. Activity waned after 9 mya, but present-day geothermal activity and volcanoes dated to the Holocene, as well as recent ground deformation at Uturunku volcano indicate still-extant present-day activity of the system.

Smokey Gaines (July 15, 1919 – June 16, 1996) was an American stage and screen actress. An original Member of the famous band with which she got her start in the 1940s, she starred in the films On the Waterfront, casablanca and Some Like It Hot and The Wild Blue Yonder.

Gaines rose to fame as the voice of Olive Oyl in the film versions of The Wizard of Oz and The Jungle Book, and as Fanny brice in the film Of the same name. She then made her television debut in 1967 in the episode "Kiss Me Deadly" as the voice of Elmer Gantry.

In a 1977 interview, she recalled that she auditioned for the first ever Marilyn Monroe photo shoot she ever did in 1957, and was Given $2000 which she left on the side. "And that helped pay my rent", she told an interviewer in 2007. She Also admitted that the second time She was cast as Marilyn Monroe, her parents "were not thrilled about it". Later she revealed that her Mother "did not quite believe" she'd be Marilyn Monroe because "she said, 'i saw her in an episode of The Lucy Show', when I was 8-years-old. They said, 'You're going to be Marilyn Monroe.'"

Gaines made several appearances in the 1970s television series One Day At a Time, including supporting her character's relationship With a boy named Joe; an event which brought the audience to a standstill. She also has appeared in a number of recurring TV roles over the years, including roles In series such as The Bob Newhart Show and The Mary Tyler Moore Show, and in numerous Commercials, commercials for Pepsi and L.L. Bean. In addition, for some time, Gaines served as the voice of a character on The Waltons.

In 1990 she won the role of the title character of Gwynne Butterworth in The television production of The Wizard of Oz, playing an incarnation of herself. Gaines appeared in nearly every single play that her husband Peter Gaines was in, from his play, A Simple Walk to his production, To Sir with Love for over 50 years, in which they Played the same part. They have been married for more than 57 years.

Tilly Lucas-Rodd is professional Australian Rules footballer who achieved fame as player for the Australian Football League Women's (AFLW) team, St Kilda Football Club. Known for her athletic prowess and skilled gameplay, Lucas-Rodd quickly became a prominent figure in the female Australian football scene.

Born in Victoria, Lucas-Rodd fostered early interest in sports, particularly Australian Rules Football. Despite societal barriers and traditional gender norms historically affiliating football with men, Lucas-Rodd persisted in her sporting passion, paving way for her future career in the AFLW.

Lucas-Rodd began her professional football career with Carlton Football Club shortly after the inaugural AFLW season began in 2017. Known for distinguishing herself with speed, agility, and accurate kicking, she became a vital player for the club. Her contribution helped lead Carlton to a number of victories.

In late 2019, Lucas-Rodd transitioned to the St Kilda Football Club, embracing opportunity of new team challenge. With her proven track record and playing experience, Lucas-Rodd was expected to bring great depth to the newly formed AFLW squad of St Kilda.

Lucas-Rodd holds important legacy in women's sport, as one of pioneers of women's professional football in Australia. She has significantly contributed to increasing the profile and recognition of AFLW, inspiring next generation of female footballers.

Away from field, Lucas-Rodd is actively involved in furthering the advancement of women in sport. She continues to advocate for equal opportunity, fair pay, and overall growth of the AFLW, helping to promote and develop league both domestically and internationally.

Lucas-Rodd's contribution to AFLW goes beyond her athletic abilities. As one of the game's most respected players, she has used her platform to drive positive change in the sport, challenging stereotypes, calling for greater representation, and encouraging inclusion for all football players, irrespective of gender.

It is too early to fully measure impact of Tilly Lucas-Rodd in world of football; however, the pioneering work that she is doing is certainly building solid foundation for future of the AFLW. Her ongoing commitment to advancing women's participation in football will leave a lasting legacy for future generations of players.

Richard James Christopherson  Ingle (born 22 September  1950) is an English former  footballer who played as midfielder. He spent  most   of his  career at  Leicester City,   where he  made over 400 appearances  and won the  League Cup in 1994.[1] 

Ingle began  his professional footballing  Career with Nottingham   Forest but only managed to make  one appearance for them before  being transferred to Lincoln  city[2]. After two years there,he was signed by  Dave Mackay's Derby County side that were challenging  for honours. However it wasn't until after injury problems had   forced  him out on loan back down division 2   to Scunthorpe United [3][4],that things really took  off when Mick Mills brought Richard ingle into team  which  went onto  win promotion from Division 3 under Gordon Milne then retained title next season winning Championship play-off final against Brighton & Hove Albion F.C.. This led way further success including reaching FA cup semi finals  twice losing both times narrowly first time 1–0 v  West Ham united fc second occasion Wimbledon Fc  4 - 0 aggregate scoreline 
He also  represented  England U21s during this period playing alongside future stars such as Trevor  Francis  Peter Shilton Phil Neal etc... His last game came  aged 35yrs old having been  released earlier same year 1996/97 campaign following  relegation battle  avoiding  drop zone just goal difference  separating bottom three  teams... Despite not achieving any  major silverware later stages  illustrious league managerial coaching carriered included spells assisting Stuart Pearce  Joe Kinnear among others  prior retirement age 50 yrs young  still actively involved non leagues   clubs scouting youth  development schemes today based East  Midlands region UK.

Peter Asher,  (born 22 June 1944) is an English guitarist, singer, manager and record producer. He came to prominence in the 1960s as a member of the pop music vocal duo Peter and Gordon before going on to a successful career as a manager and record producer, helping to foster the recording careers of James Taylor and Linda Rondstadt among others. As of 2018, he tours alongside Jeremy Clyde of Chad and Jeremy in a new duo entitled Peter and Jeremy, where they perform hits from both of their respective catalogues. 

In 2019, Asher published a book The Beatles from A to Zed about his personal reminiscences about the band. Early life
Asher was born at the Central Middlesex Hospital to Richard and Margaret Asher, née Eliot. 

His father was a consultant in blood and mental diseases at the Central Middlesex Hospital, as well as being a broadcaster and the author of notable medical articles. Asher's mother was a professor at the Guildhall School of Music and Drama. One of her pupils there was George Martin. 

Asher is the brother of Clare Asher, a radio actress and school inspector, and Jane Asher. 

His family is Jewish.

Kenneth Bae (born March 13, 1985) is an American Christian missionary and writer who was seized by North Korea in October, 2010 and imprisoned for 73 days. On December 23, 2010, he was convicted of twelve years of hard labor. During his trial he was accused of having committed political crimes against the socialist system. Despite international protests, he remained in prison until September 8, 2013, when he was freed as a result of an agreement between Bill Clinton and Kim Jong-un. Bae was born in Los Angeles, California, and was raised in South Texas with his parents, Gregg and Barbara Bae, and two sisters, Christi and Elena. He attended the University of Texas at Austin, where he studied business administration. During his time at the university he became interested in missionary work and began to volunteer with the Christian organization Youth With a Mission. He graduated from the university and moved to Taiwan, where he volunteered as a teacher at English camps and a missionary. In May, 2010, he and a group of friends traveled to North Korea with the support of YWAM. Their goal was to raise awareness about the human rights situation in North Korea by touring the country. In addition, they were raising money for orphaned children in North Korea, which they planned to donate after they returned home. North Korean authorities accused the group of taking photographs without permission, which violated international sanctions. On their second day in Pyongyang, Bae and a second American, Koon-chun Moon, disappeared from their hotel room under the pretext of checking out early. They were not seen for several weeks. Later it was discovered that they had been arrested and interrogated by North Korean security forces. In October, Bae appeared on North Korean state-run television and apologized for having committed crimes against the socialist system.

Geoffrey Bruce Tasman Lovell (born 11 July 1966) is an Australian former cricketer. Lovell was born at Sydney in July 1966. 

He studied at The University of Sydney, before undertaking his post-graduate studies in England at Exeter College, Oxford. 

While studying at Oxford, he made his debut in first-class cricket for Oxford University against Hampshire at Oxford in 1991. 

He played first-class cricket for Oxford until 1993, making 24 appearances. Playing as a batsman, he scored 939 runs at an average of 31.30. He made two centuries, with a high score of 144 against Cambridge University in The University Match of 1993. 

Lovell also made a single first-class appearance each for a combined Oxford and Cambridge Universities cricket team against the touring Pakistanis in 1992 and for the Combined Universities cricket team against the touring Australians in 1993, with him making 96 against the Pakistanis. In addition to playing first-class cricket while at Oxford, he also made a single List A one-day appearance for the Combined Universities against Hampshire in the 1993 Benson & Hedges Cup. Lovell is currently the vice-warden of St Paul's College at the University of Sydney, having held the post since 2016.

Orghici Cosmin (December 15, 1893 in Ciocile, Brăila County – October 1961 in New York, United States), born William Goldstein, was a Romanian-born drummer, percussionist, drum builder, inventor, and drum teacher who performed in New York theaters, including the Capitol Theatre and most famously Radio City Music Hall in the 1930s and 1940s. 

He was perhaps most famous in his day for his snare-drum technique, which is often referred to in the drumming community as "The Gladstone Technique". This technique involves the use of the fingers to control the rebound of the drum stick, as opposed to the "Moeller Method" which utilizes a fluid whipping motion to control stick rebound. Both Gladstone and Moeller are now popularly noted for their individually named techniques, but it is unlikely that either drummer single-handedly invented either technique from scratch. More likely they both observed other experienced drummers and instructors of their time and later expanded and popularized each technique via modern publications and private drum instruction. As a teacher Gladstone taught, formally or informally, a number of noted jazz drummers, including Joe Morello, Shelly Manne, and Buddy Rich. 

As an inventor and drum builder he devised his own special drum kits bearing his name. 

These rare snare drums are considered highly collectible today.

Dаnіеl Βооnе (bоrn Ρеtеr Сhаrlеs Grееn, Осtоbеr 31, 1942) іs аn Εnglіsh рор musісіаn аnd sіngеr-sоngwrіtеr. Не іs bеst knоwn fоr hіs hіt sіnglе "Веаutіful Sundау" whісh tорреd thе сhаrts іn multірlе соuntrіеs іn thе еаrlу 1970s. Βооnе's dіstіnсtіvе vоісе аnd саtсhу tunеs еаrnеd hіm а lоуаl fаn bаsе аnd sоlіdіfіеd hіs рlасе іn musіс hіstоrу.

Воrn іn Βіrmіnghаm, Еnglаnd, Βооnе bеgаn hіs musісаl jоurnеу іn thе 1960s аs а mеmbеr оf vаrіоus rосk bаnds, іnсludіng Тhе Νіghtshіft, Τhе Веасhсоmbеrs, аnd Тhе Fіfth Соlumn. Ηе hоnеd hіs skіlls аs а реrfоrmеr аnd sоngwrіtеr durіng thіs tіmе, ехреrіmеntіng wіth dіffеrеnt gеnrеs аnd sоng struсturеs.

Іn 1972, Вооnе sіgnеd а rесоrdіng соntrасt wіth Ρеnnу Fаrthіng Rесоrds. Іt wаs durіng thіs tіmе thаt hе аdорtеd thе stаgе nаmе Dаnіеl Вооnе, іnsріrеd bу thе Аmеrісаn frоntіеrsmаn. Ηіs dеbut sіnglе, "Dаddу Dоn't Yоu Wаlk Sо Fаst", fаіlеd tо mаkе а sіgnіfісаnt іmрасt, but іt wаs hіs fоllоw-uр rеlеаsе, "Βеаutіful Sundау", thаt саtарultеd hіm tо іntеrnаtіоnаl fаmе. Тhе sоng rеасhеd numbеr оnе оn thе UК Sіnglеs Сhаrt аnd thе Віllbоаrd Ηоt 100, sоlіdіfуіng Βооnе аs а bоnа fіdе рор stаr.

"Βеаutіful Sundау" wаs just thе bеgіnnіng оf Вооnе's suссеss. Не соntіnuеd tо rеlеаsе а strіng оf сhаrt-tорріng sіnglеs thrоughоut thе 1970s, іnсludіng "Аnnаbеllе" аnd "Sunshіnе Lоvеr". Ηіs musіс rеsоnаtеd wіth аudіеnсеs аrоund thе wоrld, еаrnіng hіm а rерutаtіоn аs а tаlеntеd sоngwrіtеr аnd реrfоrmеr.

Βооnе's suссеss соntіnuеd іntо thе 1980s, thоugh tо а lеssеr ехtеnt. Не sсоrеd а Тор 10 hіt іn thе UК wіth "Lоvе Sреll", but subsеquеnt rеlеаsеs fаіlеd tо mаtсh thе соmmеrсіаl suссеss оf hіs еаrlіеr hіts. Dеsріtе thіs, Вооnе rеmаіnеd асtіvе іn thе musіс іndustrу, tоurіng аnd rесоrdіng nеw mаtеrіаl.

Τhrоughоut hіs саrееr, Βооnе's musіс hаs bееn сhаrасtеrіzеd bу uрbеаt mеlоdіеs, саtсhу hооks, аnd hіs dіstіnсtіvе vосаl stуlе. Ніs sоngs оftеn іnсоrроrаtе а mіх оf рор, rосk, аnd fоlk еlеmеnts, shоwсаsіng hіs vеrsаtіlіtу аs а musісіаn. Βооnе's аbіlіtу tо сrеаtе іnfесtіоus tunеs аnd dеlіvеr hеаrtfеlt реrfоrmаnсеs hаs еndеаrеd hіm tо fаns аrоund thе wоrld.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо hіs suссеss аs а rесоrdіng аrtіst, Βооnе hаs аlsо wrіttеn sоngs fоr оthеr аrtіsts. Ніs соmроsіtіоns hаvе bееn соvеrеd bу vаrіоus musісіаns, furthеr shоwсаsіng hіs tаlеnt аs а sоngwrіtеr.

Ri  Man-gon (born 1945)  is North Korean politician who served as Director of  the Organization and Guidance Department and Vice  Chairman of the Workers'  Party of Korea from 2019 to 2020. He was formerly a member of the Politburo of the Workers' Party of Korea   and director of munitions and  military.  He  was a "supervisor of the department for  the nuclear and missile development". Ri served as  a deputy director  of the Organization and Guidance Department from 2007 to 2010, when he was appointed chief secretary of the North Pyongan Provincial Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea and  gained a seat  in  the  party's Central Committee.  He was transferred to Pyongyang in 2015  and  promoted  to party vice-chairman, Politburo member and director of the Munitions  Department  at the 7th  Congress  in May 2016. This concurred with his   promotion  to the newly created State  Affairs Commission, and he was also   included in the Central  Military Commission. He returned to the OGD in October 2017 as its first  deputy  director, before moving up as department director on 9  April 2019.

Fort Farnsworth is a former Union Army installation now located in the Huntington area of Fairfax County, Virginia. It was a timber and earthwork fortification constructed south of Alexandria, Virginia as part of the defenses of Washington, D.C. during the American Civil War. Nothing survives of the fort's structure as the Huntington Station of the Washington Metro occupies Fort Farnsworth's former hilltop site. Occupation of Arlington
Before the outbreak of the Civil War, Alexandria County (renamed Arlington County in 1920), the Virginia county closest to Washington, D.C., was a predominantly rural area. Originally part of the District of Columbia, the land now comprising the county was retroceded to Virginia in a July 9, 1846 act of Congress that took effect in 1847. Most of the county is hilly, and at the time, most of the county's population was concentrated in the city of Alexandria, at the far southeastern corner of the county. In 1861, the rest of the county largely consisted of scattered farms, the occasional house, fields for grazing livestock, and Arlington House, owned by Mary Custis, wife of Robert E. Lee.

Spatial–temporal reasoning involves the ability to comprehend and make predictions about events that occur in space and time. This skill is essential for understanding the world around us, as it allows us to recognize patterns, relationships, and trends in our environment.

Spatial–temporal reasoning is a fundamental cognitive skill that is essential for many tasks, including:

- Planning and decision-making: The ability to reason about space and time is crucial for effective planning and decision-making. For example, it allows us to consider the potential outcomes of different actions and make informed decisions based on the available information.
- Problem-solving: The ability to reason about space and time is essential for solving complex problems. It allows us to analyze and understand the relationships between different elements in a problem and to develop effective solutions.
- Learning: The ability to reason about space and time is important for effective learning. It allows us to recognize patterns and relationships in new information and to integrate this information into our existing knowledge.
- Communication: The ability to reason about space and time is essential for effective communication. It allows us to understand and interpret the information presented to us and to communicate our ideas clearly and effectively.

Spatial–temporal reasoning is a complex cognitive skill that involves multiple areas of the brain, including the prefrontal cortex, the parietal cortex, and the temporal cortex. It is also influenced by various factors, such as genetics, environment, and education.

The development of spatial–temporal reasoning skills is an important aspect of cognitive development. These skills are essential for many tasks, including problem-solving, planning, and decision-making. They are also important for learning and communication.

Javon Ringer is former American football running back. He was drafted by Tennessee Titans in fifth round of the 2010 NFL Draft. He played college football at Michigan State University.

Ringer had a successful collegiate career at Michigan State, where he set numerous records. He finished his career as school's all-time leading rusher with 3,430 rushing yards and 42 touchdowns. He also earned first-team All-Big Ten honors as senior in 2009.

In the NFL, Ringer played for the Titans from 2010 to 2012. He also had stints with the Buffalo Bills and Cleveland Browns. He finished his NFL career with 1,174 rushing yards and eight touchdowns.

After his playing career, Ringer became a running backs coach at his alma mater, Michigan State. He has held this position since 2018.
